## Program Information

* **Program Name:** BS Computer Science
* **University:** SZABIST University
* **Duration:** Four years (eight semesters)
* **Accreditation:** Accredited by National Computing Education & Accreditation Council (NCEAC)
* **Total Credit Hours:** 130
* **Total Courses:** 44

## Course Distribution

* **Computing Core:** 46 credit hours, 14 courses
* **Domain Core:** 18 credit hours, 6 courses
* **Domain Elective:** 21 credit hours, 7 courses
* **Mathematics and Supporting Courses:** 12 credit hours, 4 courses
* **Elective Supporting Courses:** 3 credit hours, 1 course
* **General Education Requirement:** 30 credit hours, 12 courses

## Admission Requirements

* **O-Levels:** Minimum 8 subjects, including 5 compulsory subjects (English, Urdu, Maths, Islamiat, and Pakistan Studies) and A-levels (minimum 3 subjects) / 12th Grade/Intermediate with minimum 50% marks or equivalent from a recognized institution
* **Mathematical Background:** Preferred for BS Computer Science program
* **Inter Board Committee of Chairman (IBCC) Equivalency/Attestation:** Required for O & A Levels/IB Diploma/High School Diploma/SSC & HSC or equivalent

## Fee Structure

* **Application Processing Fee:** Rs. 2,000
* **Admission Fee:** Rs. 25,000
* **Security Deposit (Refundable):** Rs. 15,000
* **Student Activity Charges:** Rs. 1,500
* **Tuition Fee (Per Semester 18 Cr Hrs):** Rs. 160,200
* **Lab Charges per Semester:** Rs. 3,000/-
* **Course Registration Fee Per Course:** Rs. 500/-
* **Examination Fee Per Course:** Rs. 500/-

## Curriculum

### First Year

* **Fall Semester:**
	+ Calculus and Analytical Geometry
	+ English Composition and Comprehension
	+ Fundamentals of Programming
	+ Lab: Fundamentals of Programming
	+ Introduction to Computer Science
	+ Lab: Introduction to Computer Science
	+ Applied Physics
	+ Lab: Applied Physics
* **Spring Semester:**
	+ Object Oriented Programming Techniques
	+ Lab: Object Oriented Programming Techniques
	+ Probability and Statistics
	+ Digital Logic Design
	+ Lab: Digital Logic Design
	+ Islamic Studies/ Humanities
	+ Pakistan Studies
	+ Communication and Presentation Skills

### Second Year

* **Fall Semester:**
	+ Data Structures and Algorithms
	+ Lab: Data Structures and Algorithms
	+ Multivariable Calculus
	+ Discrete Mathematical Structures
	+ Computer Organization and Assembly Language
	+ Lab: Computer Organization and Assembly Language
	+ HCI & Computer Graphics
	+ Lab: HCI & Computer Graphics
	+ University Elective-1
* **Spring Semester:**
	+ Database Systems
	+ Lab: Database Systems
	+ Finite Automata Theory and Formal Languages
	+ Linear Algebra
	+ Design and Analysis of Algorithms
	+ Computer Architecture

### Third Year

* **Fall Semester:**
	+ Operating Systems
	+ Lab: Operating Systems
	+ Compiler Construction
	+ Software Engineering
	+ Advance Database Management Systems
	+ Lab: Advance Database Management Systems
	+ Community Service
	+ Entrepreneurship
* **Spring Semester:**
	+ Technical and Business Writing
	+ Computer Networks
	+ Lab: Computer Networks
	+ Artificial Intelligence
	+ Lab: Artificial Intelligence
	+ CS Elective-1
	+ CS Elective-2

### Fourth Year

* **Fall Semester:**
	+ Final Year Project-I
	+ CS Elective-3
	+ CS Elective-4
	+ University Elective-2
	+ Information Security
	+ Lab: Information Security
	+ Professional Practices
* **Spring Semester:**
	+ Final Year Project-II
	+ Parallel and Distributed Computing
	+ CS Elective-5
	+ CS Elective-6
	+ CS Elective-7

## Additional Information

* **Mathematics deficiency course:** Offered to students having limited mathematical background, as identified by the relevant PM/HOD.

**Program Information**

* **Program Name:** Master of Science in Cyber Security (MS Cyber Security)
* **Duration:** 2 years
* **Credit Hours:** 30 credit hours
* **Core Courses:** 3
* **Specialized Data Science Courses:** 2
* **Thesis/Research:** 6 credit hours (mandatory)
* **Maximum Time Limit:** 4 years

**Program Description:**
The MS (Cyber Security) program is designed to give students the option to be part of innovative IT solutions. The goal of the program is to enable students to apply scientific and technological development in building a secure information society.

**Admission Requirements:**

* **Education:** 16 years of relevant education with a minimum of 50% marks/2.0 CGPA from a university recognized by HEC
* **Test Scores:** GAT (General) or HAT relevant with a minimum 50% score
* **Verification:** Last degree verification from the Higher Education Commission (HEC) is required

**Fee Structure:**

* **Processing Fee:** Rs. 2,000
* **Admission Fee:** Rs. 25,000
* **Security Deposit (refundable):** Rs. 15,000
* **Student Activity Charges:** Rs. 1,500
* **Tuition Fee (per semester, 3 courses):** Rs. 72,000
* **Course Registration Fee Per Course:** Rs. 500/-
* **Examination Fee Per Course:** Rs. 500/-
* **Final Open Defense for MS by Research Program charges:** Rs. 10,000/-

**Course Information:**

* **Core Courses:**
	+ Network Security
	+ Information Security
	+ Digital Forensics
	+ Applied Cryptograph
* **Deficiency Courses:**
	+ Programming Fundamentals (Core Programming Course)
	+ Data Structures & Algorithms or Design & Analysis of Algorithms
	+ Computer Networks
* **Elective Courses:**
	+ Network Penetration Testing and Countermeasures
	+ Security in Mobile and Wireless Networks
	+ Ethical Hacking
	+ Malware Detection and Analysis
	+ Blockchain and Crypto Assets
	+ Intrusion Detection and Firewalls
	+ Reverse Engineering and Malware Analysis
	+ Security and Privacy for the Smart Grid
	+ Machine Learning for Cyber Security
	+ Security Modelling and Analysis of Mobile Agent Systems
	+ Security in Ad Hoc Sensor Networks
	+ Security in Cloud Environment
	+ Advanced Topic in Cyber Security - I
	+ Advanced Topic in Cyber Security - II
